channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: CD Projekt Red's Hadar has moved onto the prototyping stage age, as The Witcher 4 team expands
item.content:encoded:
"Work on the project is progressing dynamically"

Project Hadar… It's a completely new game from CD Projekt Red, one that the Cyberpunk 2077 studio has been tinkering away at in the background for a while now. And yet! We know next to nothing about it, not even the genre (yes I know it's probably an RPG given CD Projekt's whole thing, I'm creating mystique here). But today was CD Projekt's most recent earnings report, so now we know… not much much, though we do have a better idea of where it's at.

CD Projekt joint CEO Michał Nowakowski shared during a presentation today that "work on the project is progressing dynamically, and the team has established the foundation of this entirely new IP. Now, we have clearly-defined distinguishing features of this world, and established key pillars that will not only provide the foundation for the game itself, but also potentially for other future products. Of course this does not mean the end of work on IP itself, which will naturally continue to evolve, and develop on many fronts over time."

"The team has established the foundations of this entirely new IP. Now, we are designing specific elements that might be included in the game, creating multiple prototypes and implementing them directly in Unreal Engine." - said @michalnowakow during FY 2025 earnings conference pic.twitter.com/jMiwsIvwW5

— CD PROJEKT IR (@CDPROJEKTRED_IR) March 19, 2026

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

While still keeping pretty tight lipped about it, Nowakowski continued to explain that the team working on Hadar is working on various prototypes, testing "specific elements" that may (or may not) make it into the final game. Essentially, it sounds like they're very early into figuring out what the game actually is, both narratively and mechanically.

Not much to go off of overall, but a brief update was offered towards The Witcher 4's progress, that being that the dev team is bigger now, up to 499 staff members, an increase of almost 50 people since October last year. There's more folks on Cyberpunk 2 now too, but only a handful comparatively. With all this talk about sequels, you can't help but wonder about that speculated upcoming Witcher 3 DLC. Shame CD Projekt didn't mention anything about that.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/cd-projekt-reds-hadar-has-moved-onto-the-prototyping-stage-age-as-the-witcher-4-team-expands
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Publisher Accused Of Leaking Subnautica 2 Release Date To Spite Co-Founders
item.content:encoded:
Yesterday, I said Subnautica 2 was free from legal drama. I spoke too soon. The newly reinstated CEO of the studio behind the upcoming underwater survival game, Unknown Worlds, claims that publisher Krafton intentionally leaked the game’s early access release window of May and potentially violated a judge’s ruling in the process.

On March 19, Game File reported that lawyers for Unknown Worlds sent a letter to Judge Will on Tuesday night shortly after IGN reported that an internal memo from Steve Papoutsis, the executive Krafton put in charge of the studio after the 2025 firings, had been sent out to the team behind Subnautica 2, announcing a May release window. Krafton later confirmed this release date with Kotaku and other outlets. But according to the letter from the ousted co-founders and newly returned CEO Ted Gill, Krafton didn’t have the authority to do this at all.

Judge Lori Will had ordered on March 16 that Gill be reinstated as CEO on that Monday morning, and only he would get to determine Subnautica 2′s launch plans. So when Papoutsis wrote and sent out that memo on Tuesday, he wasn’t even legally CEO of Unknown Worlds anymore.

“Krafton self-servingly announced the launch without any regard to its impact on the game, the team, or the community—let alone this Court’s Opinion,” said Gill and the co-founders’ lawyers in a letter to the judge reported by Game File.

“Announcing the release of a game is momentous, and it is typically accompanied by significant marketing activity, fanfare, and community coordination. And most importantly, the announcement is carefully designed to maximize excitement for the game. That entire process was supposed to be driven by Mr. Gill. However, in defiance of the Court’s Opinion, Krafton has now taken that away, further damaging the game and sowing additional confusion among the Subnautica community.”

The CEO and co-founders’ lawyers claim that Krafton ‘intentionally leaked” the release date to the press and then sent out a statement confirming it, and suggested Judge Will hold Krafton in contempt for its actions.

Krafton responds to Subnautica 2 CEO’s legal letter

Game File reports that on Wednesday, lawyers for Krafton sent their own letter to Judge Will and claimed the publisher had not violated her order.

“Papoutsis’s message was simply celebrating the [Unknown Worlds] employees’ efforts toward a past event: Krafton’s pre-Opinion determination that Subnautica 2 was ready for Early Access release. There was nothing improper about conveying the results of the milestone review or thanking the development team for their dedication and talent,” said Krafton’s legal team in its letter.

Krafton’s lawyers further clarified that Gill, as CEO, could still decide when to release Subnautica 2 and that “nothing in Papoutsis’s message alters Gill’s authority or discretion.”

Judge Will has reportedly not responded to either letter. Both sides were previously ordered to meet up later this week and submit a proposed judgment that would be in line with Judge Will’s ruling earlier this week. Gill’s letter from his legal team didn’t confirm or deny whether the newly returned CEO will stick with that allegedly leaked May release date.



https://kotaku.com/publisher-accused-of-leaking-subnautica-2-release-date-to-spite-co-founders-2000680432
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Crimson Desert Launch Pulls Huge Twitch and Steam Numbers
item.content:encoded:
Crimson Desert launches with impressive Twitch viewership and Steam player counts, even as review scores remain mixed.

Crimson Desert is the new open-world game from Pearl Abyss, and it launched on March 19. It is already the number one-streamed game on Twitch. The game currently has nearly 500,000 viewers on the major streaming platform.

On SteamDB, the game has a concurrent player count of over 131,000. It’s currently the number six-streamed software on Steam. The title is currently outperforming Marvel Rivals, ARC Raiders, Overwatch, and more.

Crimson Desert currently has a Metacritic score of 78. This is based on 98 scores ranging from several 100s to the lowest current score of 45. Insider Gaming scored the game a 7. You can check out our full review here.

Pearl Abyss’ stock price fell by nearly 30% after receiving some of the mediocre review scores. According to the Seoul Economic Daily, the market had anticipated a median score in the mid-to-high 80s, and the disappointing results seem to have negatively impacted the stock.

While it remains to be seen how the game performs, the metrics are at least high for now, and perhaps more buzz will be generated for the title, leading to positive user reviews in the future.

What do you think about the current response to Crimson Desert? Have you played the game so far? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/crimson-desert-launch-pulls-huge-twitch-and-steam-numbers/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Crimson Desert: Complete Walkthrough and Guides Hub
item.content:encoded:
Crimson Desert is Pearl Abyss’s ambitious open-world epic, taking players to the war-torn continent of Pywel. Following Kliff, the leader of the Greymane mercenaries, you will navigate political intrigue, master brutal combat, and explore a world filled with mythical creatures and ancient mysteries to restore the mercenary company after it was decimated by a surprise attack from the rival Black Bears.

In this Complete Walkthrough and Guides Hub, you will find everything you need to rebuild the Greymanes, master the complex combat system, and uncover the secrets of the Abyss.

Table of contents

  • Getting Started
  • Combat Mechanics
  • Character Progression and Skills
  • World Exploration
    • Challenges
  • Boss Strategies
  • Troubleshooting
  • Review

Getting Started

Pywel is a vast and unforgiving land where you are mostly on your own to figure out the game's intricacies. These guides will help you get started, and make you enjoy the game from the very beginning.

  • How to Expand Inventory Space
  • How to Change Time of Day
  • How to Earn Money Fast

Combat Mechanics

Combat in Crimson Desert blends traditional swordplay with wrestling-style grapples and elemental magic for an intricate yet explosive combination.

  • Best Early Weapon: How to Use the Broken Spear and Its Overpowered Evasive Slash Counter
  • Use This Broken Skill To Wipe Out Large Groups Of Enemies

Character Progression and Skills

Crimson Desert doesn't feature a traditional XP system, as characters are enhanced with items obtained through exploration and discovery.

  • Best Skills to Learn First For Kliff
  • Best Stats to Level Up First
  • How to Respec and Reset Skill Points
  • How to Summon Companions and Swap Characters

World Exploration

The continent of Pywel is divided into five massive, seamless regions, filled with all sorts of secrets to discover, puzzles to solve and challenges to complete

  • How to Unlock Fast Travel and Find Abyss Nexuses
  • Fast Travel Trick: How to Teleport Anywhere Using Character Swapping

Challenges

  • How to Clear the Sliding Down the Slope Challenge (Coming soon!)

Boss Strategies

Crimson Desert features over 70 unique bosses that will put your skills to the test with varied movesets that can make short work of your characters.

  • How to Beat the Marni's Excavatron (Coming soon!)
  • How to Beat the Reed Devil (Coming soon!)

Crafting & Management

Crimson Desert features a crafting system that allows you to create and enhance your own gear, as well as deep management of the Greymanes camp.

In-depth guides on crafting and camp management will go live soon

Troubleshooting

Although Crimson Desert has few bugs and issues, some may still creep up in some versions of the game.

  • How to Fix DualSense Controller Icons On PC (Bluetooth Fix)

Review

Is Crimson Desert a game worth your time? Check out my review to learn why the game is, in my opinion, among the best open-world games of the current generation.

All guides are currently based on a 30+ hours playthrough in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version.

Last updated: March 19, 2026

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/crimson-desert-complete-walkthrough-and-guides-hub/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Crimson Desert Combat Too Hard? Use This Broken Skill to Wipe Out Large Groups Of Enemies
item.content:encoded:
If you find yourself constantly overwhelmed by enemies in Crimson Desert, you aren’t alone. The early game, in particular, is extremely difficult, so much so that the huge enemy numbers Kliff has to fight as he attempts to restore the Greymanes can make the entire experience very frustrating

While the game asks a lot out of you at the beginning of the game, there is a very simple yet effective way to even the odds against this huge enemy numbers: the Blinding Flash Finisher skill.

How to Unlock the Blinding Flash Finisher

Unlocking Blinding Flash Finisher is really easy, and can be done as soon as you learn Blinding Flash in the early game.

Requirements:

  1. Blinding Flash Lv. 1.
  2. 1 Abyss Artifact

The Crowd Eraser

Reminiscent of the Ultimate Technique from the Ninja Gaiden series, the Blinding Flash Finisher is one of the best tools for managing crowds. It is so dominant that it continues to be a great option for a long time, even after you have learned other powerful skills.

To use Blinding Flash Finisher, you have to:

  • Hold L1 + R1 (PlayStation) or LB + RB (Xbox) to activate Blade Flash
  • Use the left stick to blind as many enemies as possible
  • Press R2 (PlayStation) or RT (Xbox) multiple times to unleash a series of quick attacks against all the blinded enemies

There are a few reasons why Blinding Flash Finisher is so good, it is effectively broken against crowds.

  • When using the skill, the world slows down, essentially preventing enemies from damaging Kliff
  • Kliff automatically dashes from one enemy to the next as long as he has Spirit to unleash the final attack.
  • Blinding Flash Finisher's final slash unleashes a massive shockwave that knocks back any survivors, preventing you from being assaulted immediately once the animation ends.

In addition to the above, it is very easy to spam Blinding Flash Finisher at almost all times. As damage is so high, you will likely kill several enemies during the flurry, and, as attacks and kills restore Spirit, the cost of using the skill is almost nullified.

In my playthrough, I struggled consistently in the early game against the armies of enemies pouring out of occupied territories, and even bringing another character into the fray didn't help much. Eventually, I stopped fighting fair, as the AI certainly does. After unlocking the Blinding Flash Finisher and combining it with the Spear's Evasive Slash Counter Stance, I stopped struggling.

Try this skill out at least once and you will see: it instantly makes combat more enjoyable, giving you the breathing room needed to experiment with every other skill in Kliff's arsenal

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our Crimson Desert Complete Walkthrough and Guides Hub.

Guide based on a 30+ hours playthrough in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/crimson-desert-combat-too-hard-use-this-broken-skill-to-wipe-out-large-groups-of-enemies/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Crimson Desert – How to Change Time of Day
item.content:encoded:
In Crimson Desert, changing the time of day is as important as it is in every open-world game, if not even more. With some bounty targets only being out and about only at certain hours, and with nights being dark enough to impede your visibility, there are times when you may want to move time forward.

Table of contents

  • How to Change Time of Day
  • Can You Always Change the Time of Day?

How to Change Time of Day

Unlike the vast majority of open-world games, you cannot change the time of day in Crimson Desert by picking an option from the main menu.

To advance time, you must locate a Bonfire anywhere in the world and do the following:

  1. Approach the fire.
  2. Press L1 (PS5) or LB (Xbox) to focus on the fire.
  3. Move your cursor to the bonfire icon and press X (PS5) or A (Xbox) to access the "Wait" menu.
  4. Choose the duration. You can choose to advance time by 3, 6, or 12 hours.

While finding a bonfire every time you want to advance time can be annoying, the game keeps track of all your discovered bonfires on the map. They are identified by a small fire icon.

Alternatively, you can also use beds to rest and thus change the time of day.

Can You Always Change the Time of Day?

Unlike many other open-world games, you cannot spam the Wait mechanics to skip days at a time in Crimson Desert. The game features a rest cooldown, which makes it so your character needs to actually be fatigued from travel, combat, or the passage of time before they can rest again at a bonfire. If the option is greyed out, you'll need to head back out into Pywel for a while before the next nap.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our Crimson Desert Complete Walkthrough and Guides Hub.

Guide based on a 30+ hours playthrough in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/crimson-desert-how-to-change-time-of-day/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Crimson Desert – Best Stats to Level Up First
item.content:encoded:
In Crimson Desert, Kliff’s physical growth is dictated by three core attributes: Health, Spirit, and Stamina. As you increase these stats by using Abyss Artifacts, which cannot be refunded without another rare type of Abyss Artifact, rather than traditional XP grinding, how you level up certain stats will determine how well you will do as you take your first steps in Pywell to restore the Greymanes.

Table of contents

  • All Upgradable Stats
  • Stats Leveling Up Priority Ranking
    • 1. Stamina
    • 2. Health
    • 3. Spirit

All Upgradable Stats

All playable characters in Crimson Desert have three stats that can be upgraded via the Skill Tree using Abyss Artifacts.

  • Health: Your total hit points. This determines how much damage you can take before being defeated.
  • Spirit: This resource is consumed by certain skills, such as Palm Strike. It is primarily replenished by attacking enemies, but also with items.
  • Stamina: The most versatile stat. It is consumed by sprinting, climbing, heavy combat strings (R2/RT) and most other melee skills. It also dictates how much damage you can block.

Stats Leveling Up Priority Ranking

1. Stamina

Without a doubt, Stamina is the most important stat to prioritize early on for the following reasons.

  • Exploration is the heart of the Crimson Desert experience. Higher stamina allows you to climb for longer, and generally traverse the map with fewer interruptions.
  • Because you are often pitted against massive groups of enemies, blocking is essentia. A small Stamina bar will see your guard broken often, leaving you with no stamina for a short time to perfom anything besides your basic light attack string.
  • Many powerful skills, including a broken Spear skill that can make short of work of mobs and bosses, consume stamina. Advanced abilities, such as Aerial Swing (Axiom Force Level 3), require a high total maximum Stamina to even activate as well.

2. Health

While Stamina is crucial to enhance early on for its traversal and combat applications, Health is a more straightforward stat.

  • Given the aggressive nature of enemies and the considerable power of bosses, having more Health is necessary to survive without heavily relying on healing items. However, it takes second place in this priority ranking because most early-game damage can be mitigated through movement and blocking, which becomes more effective with higher Stamina.

3. Spirit

Spirit is the least vital stat to level up for the early game.

  • As Spirit is restored simply by attacking, you will rarely find yourself out of it if you don't abuse certain abilities. The starting pool is generally enough to handle the starting skills without needing a permanent boost.

NOTE: It is important to note that any stat improvement made for Kliff is also applied to the other playable characters. As such, you do not need to worry about Damiane and Oonkga remaining behind.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our Crimson Desert Complete Walkthrough and Guides Hub.

Guide based on a 30+ hours playthrough in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/crimson-desert-best-stats-to-level-up-first/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marathon's Cryo Archive drops tomorrow and it sounds like almost every aspect of it will be a pain in the shell
item.content:encoded:
Well, you all did it, Cryo Archive has been unlocked in Marathon. Bungie detailed the new mode in a blog post earlier today, where it shared an important thing to keep in mind with the new map: it's only available to attempt on weekends. Aye, you'll need to forgo any social plans you have out in the real world to tackle this one.

At the very least, "weekend" sounds like it'll include Friday, as the new map actually goes live tomorrow, March 20th. But there's another important thing to keep in mind, and that's the fact it also sounds like Cryo Archive can't be taken on solo. "It's designed to be taken on by a team of Runners, so you'll need a crew of three," the blog post explains. This can of course be with a couple of mates, but if you typically fly solo, you'll have to fill your team up with randos for this one.

Anyway, what's this map actually like? "In game, Cryo Archive takes you through six interconnected wings with a central hub. Traverse from wing-to-hub and back again in search of frozen Vaults containing powerful loot and dark secrets. Battle your way through intense UESC security, decked out Runner crews, and a ship that seems hellbent on keeping you out."

It is specifically designed to be challenging, more so than normal, and will apparently take a lot of runs to fully learn. Bungie also likens Cryo Archive to raids, saying it has similarly designed "puzzle solving and layered mechanics wrapped around brutal extraction stakes." Part of this will involve gaining certain levels of security clearance, which in a run will let you access more and more rooms.

There's also seven vaults to try and get into, with specific challenges or requirements in place you'll have to see to first. Once you're in, you'll find things like high-end containers, gold weapons, and "a path toward assembling a DN~~~%^&*()8167f48f-e253-4e2f-be4c-2077b646d531+{}:"<>?~~ that leads deeper into the Marathon's secrets and towa~~~~%^&*()_+{}:"<>?~~~~~~~."

Getting out of Cryo Archive will apparently be tougher than normal too, but figuring out how to get off will be up to you. And for quest-enjoyers, you'll have access to seven unique contracts to try out. Sounds pretty full on! Better you than me, I'll stick to Peggle.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/marathons-cryo-archive-drops-tomorrow-and-it-sounds-like-almost-every-aspect-of-it-will-be-a-pain-in-the-shell
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New Steam Hit Is All About Running A Rental Store In The ’90s And It’s Rad
item.content:encoded:
In 2026, and streaming sucks. Movies and shows are split across a dozen services and apps that are all getting worse and more expensive. But it wasn’t always like this. There was a time, years and years ago, when you’d head to a building to rent a movie. You’d grab a VHS tape, maybe a soda and a candy bar too, and then head home to watch the latest flick. You can’t go back in time, but you can play Retro Rewind – Video Store Simulator, a newly released Steam game that lets you run one of these old-school movie rental shops.

Out now on PC, Retro Rewind is yet one more of those “store simulator” games that have been flooding Steam for a few years now. I’ve played many of them, and most of them lack any personality and feel like they were spit out by a factory. That’s not the case with Retro Rewind, which is filled with charm and character.

Every part of this store sim feels like it was made by people who cared. It also feels like the devs grew up going to places like Blockbuster and other local rental stores, as Retro Rewind captures the vibes perfectly. All the fake movie covers you encounter ooze the mid-’90s. You can also decorate your store just like most rental places were decorated back then, with movie posters and cheesy carpet designs featuring popcorn buckets. Customers will occasionally bring back movies late, and you’ll have to charge a fee (or not), and some people even break tapes, forcing you to toss them into the trash. RIP. And yes, throughout the day, you’ll have to step away and rewind returned VHS tapes before putting them back out on the shelves.

Like all of these store sims, the basic loop has you starting with nothing and slowly building up your store day by day as you get more shelves, better movies, and new decor, soon even hiring employees to help run the place as it expands. Doing all of this in Retro Rewind is never a slog. For example, stocking movies is a treat as you get to hold up to 10 at a time and can just drop them if you want. And you should do that at least once because it makes a wonderful noise. Every tape is a physical object that makes a satisfying plastic thwack when it lands in the return box, on the floor, or on the counter when someone plops it down for you to scan at the register.

Retro Rewind also features plenty of events and other things that can change up the day-to-day flow of running a rental shop. Weekends are busier than usual. Holidays and weather can affect what kind of movies people want. Events in the town can lead to crowds. And new releases, while expensive to buy, can help bring more people in and cost more to rent, making it easier to rack up some cash to buy more shelves or more movies.

Oh, and keep an eye out because every so often, a shady figure will appear behind your shop with some bootleg movies and porn. These are cheaper than the stuff you get from more legitimate avenues, but some customers might not want to deal with seeing them on the same shelf alongside the movies they came to rent.

I’m only a few hours into Retro Rewind, and I’m excited to get back to my rental place and help customers find a good movie while managing the phone and restocking films. Plus, I want to see what new movies are coming and expand my store so I can rent even more movies to more people.

Sure, in a lot of ways, I’m just working a job, but it’s a job that basically doesn’t exist anymore. Rental stores are dead. Streaming killed them. That makes me sad. But at least Rental Rewind is here to provide me with a digital time machine that take me back to a time when movies came in big plastic boxes and weren’t suggested to you via some AI algorithm.



https://kotaku.com/retro-rewind-rental-store-vhs-blockbuster-game-steam-review-2000680409
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
